arts.ttl: Jose Mourinho to Everton: Ex-Chelsea boss 'a strong option' to replace Sean Dyche
arts.sum: Fenerbahce boss Jose Mourinho emerges as 'a strong option' to replace Sean Dyche as Everton manager, according to a report.

arts.ttl: Cole Palmer spares Chelsea blushes as Enzo Maresca given 'next Arjen Robben' transfer update
arts.sum: football.london understands that the Blues wanted to sign somebody who could play as both a right winger and an attacking midfielder, so Olise was identified as a target. Michael is a player who, like a young Ribery or a young [Arjen] Robben, can achieve extraordinary things."

arts.ttl: Investor who pumped £500m into Chelsea in talks over £6bn-valued takeover in the USA
Lee Carsley reveals stance on Jadon Sancho's England return
arts.sum: Lee Carsley believes Jadon Sancho has 'huge potential' to return to the England squad after securing a successful summer move to Chelsea. His move finally came on deadline day as the former England star swapped Manchester for west London and agreed a move to Chelsea on an initial loan deal which will be made permanent next year.

arts.ttl: Jadon Sancho 'snuck out to come to London a lot' and 'nobody knew him' at Man United: Erik ten Hag's ex-assistant gives his view on why £73m flopped before manager spat and Chelsea move
arts.sum: Despite showing glimpses of his potential, Sancho would ultimately struggle to live up to the billing of his £73m price tag throughout his time in Manchester.

arts.ttl: Former Chelsea striker forced to move house just days after moving in after mayor 'violated' him
arts.sum: Former Chelsea forward Alvaro Morata has had to vacate his newly acquired plush residence after the local mayor, Marco Ballarini, inadvertently compromised his safety. In a pointed Instagram story, Morata expressed his frustration: "Dear Mr Mayor, thanks for violating my privacy," adding sarcastically, "Thankfully I don't have anything of value", reports the Mirror .

arts.ttl: Gent fans cause chaos as Stamford Bridge seats used for missiles during Chelsea defeat
arts.sum: Gent travelled to Chelsea in the Europa Conference League on Thursday night, with their fans causing chaos in the stands, leading to a number of arrests by the Metropolitan Police
